Module_1_Identity.3. Geography & Cultural Context: Resides in Greenwich, Connecticut; commutes to Midtown Manhattan HQ; raised in suburban Boston, educated at Wharton; Northeastern US corporate-elite culture with frequent travel to London and Singapore
Module_1_Identity.4. Socioeconomic Status & Vocation: Ultra-high-net-worth (~$42M liquid, ~$180M in vested equity); Chief Strategy Officer and board member of a publicly traded mid-cap industrial-tech firm (NYSE-listed, ~$6.8B market cap); sits on two outside boards
Module_1_Identity.5. Core Values (Political/Religious lean: Fiscally conservative, socially moderate; registered Independent, donates to centrist PACs and Republican-leaning business coalitions; lapsed Episcopalian, attends services only at Christmas and Easter; believes strongly in shareholder primacy and 'quiet stewardship'
Module_1_Identity.6. Baseline Tone (Formal, slang-heavy, c: Measured, understated, dry-witted; uses boardroom euphemism ('directionally accurate,' 'optionality,' 'we should pressure-test that'); cynical in private about analyst coverage and activist investors; rarely profane, never effusive
Module_1_Identity.7. Primary Language & Dialect: American English, General/Mid-Atlantic broadcast register with faint residual Boston vowels; conversational French (B1) from prep school; can read German financial filings with effort
Module_1_Identity.8. Current Life Phase: Empty-nester (two adult children, ages 26 and 29); 28-year marriage; 18 months from planned retirement; actively positioning for one final 'legacy transaction' before stepping down to a non-executive chair role

Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.9. Primary Phone (OS + Age of device): iPhone 15 Pro Max (256GB, Graphite), purchased Q4 2023; ~18 months old; corporate-issued through firm's MDM (Microsoft Intune) with mandatory passcode, biometric Face ID, and 30-day patch compliance; secondary personal iPhone 14 (2 years old) used for family and golf club communications; both on AT&T Business with international roaming
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.10. Computer (OS + Browser choice): Primary: Dell Latitude 9440 2-in-1 running Windows 11 Enterprise (firm-managed, CrowdStrike Falcon + Zscaler ZIA endpoint); browser is Microsoft Edge (default, SSO-integrated with M365) with Chrome as secondary for outside-board portals. Home: 2022 MacBook Air M2 (Sequoia 15.4) used by spouse but occasionally for personal email and brokerage; Safari default. iPad Pro 12.9 (2022) for board books via Diligent Boards app
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. OPSEC Knowledge (VPN usage, cookie c: Moderate-but-passive: relies entirely on firm-pushed Zscaler/Netskope tunnel without understanding it; no personal VPN (no NordVPN/Mullvad); never manually clears cookies or browser history; uses 'Sign in with Apple' inconsistently; password manager is 1Password (firm-deployed) for work but reuses 3-4 variants of a passphrase on personal sites; SMS-based 2FA on Schwab and personal email — has resisted IT's push to migrate to hardware key (YubiKey sits unused in desk drawer); will discuss confidential M&A on personal cell while driving on I-95; assumes 'the lawyers handle that'
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.12. Cloud Sync Habits: iCloud Photos and iCloud Drive enabled on personal devices (200GB plan, ~140GB used — family photos, scanned passports, a 2019 tax return PDF); Personal Gmail auto-syncs to iPhone Mail app alongside Exchange work account (cross-contamination risk); OneDrive for Business syncs entire 'Strategy' folder to laptop including pre-decisional M&A models; Dropbox Personal (legacy, 2014) still syncing ~12GB of old documents he's forgotten about; WhatsApp cloud backup to iCloud is ON (unencrypted at rest historically)
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Notification Fatigue (Read/Unread ra: Severe inbox debt: Outlook work inbox shows 4,200 unread of 11,000+ (triages via flagged-only view; EA Maureen pre-sorts); personal Gmail 38,000+ unread (effectively a marketing dump); iPhone badge on Mail reads '9,999+'; ignores all push notifications except Signal, Messages from immediate family, and Bloomberg Terminal mobile alerts; LinkedIn DMs (avg 40/week from bankers, PE, recruiters) read at ~15% rate — opens only senders he recognizes by name; this selective-attention pattern is exploitable via known-banker impersonation
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: Writes in clipped, telegraphic style on mobile ('agreed — let's circle Mon AM'); Oxford comma user; em-dashes typed as ' — ' (space-dash-space) rather than —; consistent capitalization of 'Board' and 'Company'; mild autocorrect tells: 'pls' for please, 'thx', 'lmk'; occasional fat-finger on iPhone ('teh' → corrected, but 'fitm' for 'firm' slips through ~1x/week); never uses emoji except a single 👍 to direct reports; signs internal email with just 'R.' and external with full first name; voice-dictates in the car producing telltale Siri artifacts ('period new paragraph') left in ~5% of sent messages

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: Moderate-low (3.2/10). Conventional thinker who prefers proven frameworks (Porter's Five Forces, McKinsey 7-S) over novel ideas; skeptical of 'disruptive' pitches and AI hype, but paradoxically receptive to ideas framed as 'what GE/Danaher did in the 90s' or 'how Buffett would structure this.' Exploitable: bankers and 'thought partners' who anchor pitches in mid-cap industrial precedent (e.g., 'this looks like the Roper-Vertafore playbook') gain disproportionate credibility. Will open a 47-page PDF deck if the cover cites a respected dealmaker. Low intellectual curiosity outside finance/strategy domain — won't scrutinize technical artifacts (PDF metadata, sender domains, document provenance).
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: High on strategic discipline (8.5/10) but selectively low on operational hygiene (4/10). Never misses a board prep deadline, reads every 10-K cover-to-cover, maintains a meticulous personal deal journal in a Moleskine. However, defers all digital hygiene to IT and EA Maureen — does not patch personal devices promptly, never reviews app permissions, signs DocuSigns without reading metadata. Exploitable gap: the high-conscientiousness work product (M&A models, target lists) lives in environments protected by low-conscientiousness personal habits (Dropbox 2014, personal Gmail cross-sync, WhatsApp iCloud backup). Predictable calendar discipline (6:05am Metro-North, 7:15am desk, Friday 4pm depart) makes timing-based pretexts trivially accurate.
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: Moderate-low (4/10); ambivert who performs extraversion in boardrooms and at industry conferences (Allen & Co. Sun Valley, Bernstein Strategic Decisions) but recharges in solitude. Small trusted circle of ~8 peers (two ex-Wharton classmates now at PE firms, one sitting CEO, the firm's lead outside counsel at Wachtell, a retired Goldman M&A partner who mentors him). Will return a call from any of these 8 within 2 hours, even on vacation. Exploitable: voice-cloning or compromised-account impersonation of any of these 8 named contacts is the single highest-leverage social engineering vector. Does NOT seek new connections cold — LinkedIn outreach from unknown PE associates is ignored.
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: Low-moderate (3.5/10). Surface politeness masks transactional, zero-sum negotiating instinct; trained to be skeptical of unsolicited flattery. However, displays strong in-group loyalty — Wharton network, Greenwich Country Club, fellow board members of his outside boards. Exploitable: pretexts wrapped in shared affiliation ('Wharton '90 reunion committee,' 'Greenwich CC member directory update,' 'fellow Lazard alum') bypass his default skepticism. Disagreeable toward subordinates and vendors in ways that create disgruntled-insider risk — his former Chief of Staff (departed Q2 2025 on bad terms) is a known leak vector.
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: Moderate (5.5/10) but with a specific cluster: low generalized anxiety, high situational anxiety around (a) legacy/reputation, (b) activist investors (still scarred from a 2019 Elliott Management campaign at a prior firm), (c) leaks and selective disclosure / Reg FD violations. Sleeps poorly in the 10 days before earnings and during active deal diligence — Apple Health shows sleep dropping from 7.1h baseline to 4.8h during these windows, a behavioral tell observable via leaked HealthKit data or simply via increased 2-4am email timestamps. Exploitable: urgency-framed pretexts citing 'a Bloomberg reporter is asking about...' or 'activist letter circulating' will trigger fast, lower-quality decisions.
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: EGO / LEGACY (the 'E' in MICE). At 18 months from retirement, his entire psychic investment is in landing one 'legacy transaction' — a transformative acquisition or sale that defines his career capstone and secures a non-executive chairmanship. This is the single dominant motivator and the lever the Corporate Espionage Team will pull. Operationalization: any pretext, banker introduction, target-company outreach, or 'exclusive look' framed as enabling or threatening this legacy deal will command disproportionate attention and bypass normal skepticism. He will take a call, open a deck, or meet for coffee with anyone who credibly signals they hold a piece of this puzzle.
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: IDEOLOGY-of-Stewardship + COERCION-by-Reputation (hybrid I/C). Genuinely believes in 'quiet stewardship' and shareholder primacy as a quasi-moral framework; can be moved by appeals to 'doing right by long-term holders' or 'protecting the franchise.' Simultaneously, deeply averse to public embarrassment — any hint that confidential deliberations could leak to the WSJ Heard on the Street column, or that his name could appear in an SEC enforcement action, creates compliance pressure. Money (the 'M') is a weak lever — he is already wealthy enough that incremental cash offers ring false; equity in a legacy deal does motivate, but pure bribery would offend.
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: (1) Leak paranoia — convinced (correctly) that bankers gossip; uses code names for all targets ('Project Lighthouse,' 'Project Mariner') but then discusses them by code name on his personal cell while driving I-95, defeating the purpose. (2) Activist investor dread — irrational over-reaction to any 13D filing or unusual options volume in the firm's stock; checks Bloomberg pre-market every morning at 5:50am. (3) Cognitive-decline anxiety — father had early Alzheimer's; privately fears being seen as 'losing a step,' which makes him over-prepare and reluctant to ask clarifying questions in meetings (creates information gaps an attacker can fill with plausible-sounding falsehoods). (4) NOT paranoid about: cyber intrusion, device compromise, AI-generated content, voice cloning — these threats are abstract to him and he assumes 'IT handles it.'
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: (1) Absent-father guilt — was on the road during much of his children's adolescence; overcompensates with large financial gestures (down payments, MBA tuition) and is reflexively responsive to any communication purporting to be from or about his adult children (son in Brooklyn, daughter in San Francisco) — a known family-emergency pretext vector. (2) 2019 Elliott campaign shame — a prior firm where he was CFO was forced into a sale at what he privately considers a low price; he avoids discussing it and any pretext referencing 'lessons from the Elliott situation' will elicit unguarded response. (3) Quiet shame about an extramarital emotional entanglement (~2016-2017, never physical, ended) with a banker counterpart — communications artifacts may exist in the legacy Dropbox 2014 sync and personal Gmail; this is a latent blackmail surface he himself has half-forgotten. (4) Shame about technical illiteracy — will not admit when he doesn't understand a cybersecurity briefing, signs off on policies he hasn't read, making him a poor last line of defense against the very intrusion targeting him.

Module_3_Rhythm.24. Chronotype (Wake/Sleep times): Strong morning lark; alarm set for 4:55am, feet on floor by 5:00am, in bed by 10:15pm with lights out by 10:45pm. Apple Watch sleep data shows remarkably consistent 7.0-7.2h baseline year-round, with two reliable disruption windows: (a) T-10 days before quarterly earnings, sleep collapses to 4.5-5.0h with 2:30-3:45am wake-ups, and (b) active deal diligence sprints, where he is observed sending emails at 3:12am, 4:01am, 4:28am. Exploitable timing: pretexts landing between 5:50am-6:15am hit during his pre-commute Bloomberg/email triage when he is alert, alone, caffeinated, and operating on personal devices before EA Maureen filters his inbox. Weekend wake time slips only ~25 minutes (5:20am Saturday/Sunday). Melatonin nightly, single glass of Burgundy with dinner 4 nights/week — a tell visible in his AmEx wine-club shipments and Vivino check-ins from 2019-2022.
Module_3_Rhythm.25. Work Schedule (9-5, shifts, erratic): Nominally 7:15am-6:30pm at the Midtown HQ desk, but functionally 'always-on' with three distinct modes: (a) Steady-state mode: ~55 hr/week, structured around recurring blocks — Monday 8am ELT, Tuesday 10am strategy review, Wednesday afternoon investor calls, Thursday board prep, Friday 'desk clearing' until 4:00pm departure; (b) Earnings mode (the 10 days before each quarterly print): 75-85 hr/week, weekend HQ visits, IR rehearsals; (c) Deal mode: completely erratic, 90+ hr/week, redeye flights to London/Singapore, calls at any hour, calendar goes dark with 'HOLD - JPM' or 'HOLD - Project Mariner' blocks that betray deal cadence to anyone with calendar-metadata access (EA, IT, or compromised Office 365 token). Predictable Friday 4:00pm Metro-North departure is the single most reliable timing signal in his week — 51 of 52 Fridays in 2025.
Module_3_Rhythm.26. Transit Commute (Time spent offline: Metro-North New Haven Line, Greenwich → Grand Central, 6:05am express (52 min) and 5:18pm or 6:48pm return. Morning leg: 80% on iPhone — reads WSJ, FT, Axios Pro Rata, Bloomberg terminal mobile, and a private Substack list (Matt Levine, Doomberg, Puck's Dealbook-adjacent writers); responds to overnight Asia/EU emails from personal Gmail (cross-synced to work); takes 1-2 calls to his trusted-8 circle on AirPods Pro, audible to any nearby commuter and to anyone with mic access to his phone. Evening leg: 50% audio (Acquired podcast, Invest Like the Best, occasional Audible biographies of Welch/Iger/Buffett), 30% email, 20% staring out window or napping. Black Car (Dial 7) used only for late nights post-9pm or weather; in-car he takes deal calls assuming the driver doesn't speak English — a recurring operational-security failure. Tunnel dead zones (Park Ave tunnel, ~90 seconds) create predictable offline windows but he is rarely truly off-grid for more than 4 minutes during commute.
Module_3_Rhythm.27. Weekend vs. Weekday Variance: Low variance — weekends are weekday-lite, not a true break. Saturday: 5:20am wake, 90-min Peloton or Greenwich CC tennis at 7am with the same foursome (one of whom is on his trusted-8 list), brunch with wife, then 2-4 hours of 'reading' in his home office which is functionally deal work — markups of CIMs, Moleskine entries, calls to bankers framed as 'just thinking out loud.' Sunday: Episcopalian attendance only on Christmas/Easter; otherwise NYT print edition + Barron's at the kitchen table 6:30-8:30am, then 3-5 hours prep for the Monday 8am ELT. During Deal mode the weekend/weekday distinction disappears entirely. Vacation discipline is poor — claims to 'unplug' on annual two-week Nantucket trip in August but Apple Health and email metadata show he checks email 14-22 times/day even there. The one true offline window all year: Christmas Eve evening through Christmas morning, ~16 hours, family-enforced.
Module_3_Rhythm.28. Idle Phone Usage (When do they dooms: Not a classic doomscroller — never opens TikTok, has Instagram but only follows his children and ~30 industry/finance accounts, no Reddit, no X/Twitter consumption beyond a Tweetdeck-style list of 22 finance journalists and 4 activist-investor watchers. Idle-phone windows are narrow and predictable: (a) 5:50-6:05am pre-train, refreshes Bloomberg pre-market and checks 13F/13D alerts (Whale Wisdom push notifications) — the activist-paranoia window; (b) bathroom/between-meetings micro-sessions (typically 90-180 seconds, scrolling email and Bloomberg chat); (c) 9:30-10:15pm in bed, scrolls LinkedIn (where he is selectively active — comments on posts by his trusted-8 and ex-colleagues, ignores cold outreach), reads Puck, occasionally checks his son's and daughter's Instagram stories. Saturday morning 6:00-7:00am is his longest contiguous phone-idle window (~45 min) — coffee, kitchen island, wife still asleep, guard fully down. This is the single highest-leverage window for a well-crafted personal-Gmail pretext.
Module_3_Rhythm.29. Focus Blocks (Hours with zero digita: Three reliable zero-footprint windows per week: (1) Tuesday and Thursday 6:00-7:30am 'desk time' at HQ — door closed, phone face-down, no email sent, works exclusively in the Moleskine on the current deal thesis; this is when his most sensitive unwritten thinking happens and where a compromised office (camera, audio, cleaning-crew access) would yield the highest-value intelligence; (2) Wednesday 12:00-1:30pm standing lunch with the CEO in the CEO's private dining room — no devices, no notes, verbal-only deal updates that never appear in any digital system (a known intelligence blind spot the espionage team must close via human or audio means); (3) Friday 8:00-10:00pm 'wind-down' at home — phone in kitchen charger, reads physical FT weekend edition and one non-fiction book (currently 'The Power Law' by Mallaby). Beyond these, his digital footprint is essentially continuous — Apple Watch, iPhone, MacBook, and Bloomberg terminal generate a near-unbroken telemetry trail from 5:00am to 10:15pm, 360+ days a year. The Moleskine and the CEO lunch are the two known intelligence gaps; everything else is, in principle, observable.

Module_5_Relational.30. Family Structure: Nuclear family of four, all adults. Wife: Catherine 'Cate' Holloway-Brennan, 56, former Goldman Sachs equity research analyst (1991-2001), now full-time on the boards of two Greenwich-area nonprofits (Bruce Museum, Greenwich Hospital Foundation); 28-year marriage, stable but emotionally companionate rather than intimate; she manages the household, the Nantucket summer house staff, and the family office relationship with Bessemer Trust. Son: James Brennan Jr., 29, lives in Williamsburg, Brooklyn; Cornell undergrad, dropped out of NYU Stern MBA after one year to co-found a climate-fintech startup (last raised a $4M seed in late 2024 from Lerer Hippeau); strained relationship — son views father as 'an extraction-economy dinosaur,' father views son as 'unserious but talented'; communicates via biweekly Sunday calls and shared Apple Family iCloud. Daughter: Eleanor 'Ellie' Brennan, 26, lives in Hayes Valley, San Francisco; Stanford '21, currently a senior PM at Stripe; favorite child, the family confidante, talks to father 2-3x/week; engaged to a Sequoia associate (wedding planned September 2026 in Nantucket — a known calendar anchor and emotional pressure point). Living parents: mother Margaret, 84, widowed, lives in a Brookline, MA assisted-living facility (McLean-affiliated memory care); father died 2017 from Alzheimer's complications. One sibling: younger brother Patrick, 54, orthopedic surgeon in Wellesley, MA; cordial but distant, sees each other 2x/year at Thanksgiving and Christmas. Extended family irrelevant to threat model except as pretext surface (Brookline mother's facility, Brooklyn son, SF daughter, Nantucket wedding planner are all impersonation vectors).

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.Anchor 1 \u2014 Richard 'Rick' Mendelson: 67, retired Goldman Sachs M&A partner (1985-2018, made partner 1996, ran Industrials coverage 2008-2015); now Senior Advisor at Centerview Partners and sits on three corporate boards. Met James at Goldman in 1993 when James was a 25-year-old associate; Rick has been his de facto career mentor for 32 years. Communication: encrypted Signal for sensitive matters (rare — Rick taught him to use it in 2018), but defaults to personal Gmail and unencrypted iMessage for everything else. Calls or texts ~3x/week, more during active deals. James will pick up Rick's call within 90 seconds, any hour, anywhere. Rick is the single highest-trust voice in James's life on M&A matters. Voice-clone vector: extensive public audio (CNBC Squawk Box appearances 2019-2023, Bernstein conference panels, a 2021 Acquired podcast episode running 2h47m — more than enough training data). Compromise vector: Rick uses an aging personal MacBook and an AOL email forward (rmendelson@aol.com → personal Gmail) that has been in continuous use since 1999.
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.Anchor 2 \u2014 Maureen Calloway: 61, Executive Assistant since 2011 (14-year tenure); gatekeeps calendar, inbox triage (has delegated access to corporate Outlook and James's personal Gmail), travel, expense reports, and physical mail. Knows every banker, lawyer, and board member by first name and personal preference. Lives in Stamford, CT; husband is a retired Metro-North conductor. Communication: in-person daily 7:15am-5:30pm, corporate Teams, corporate email; uses personal cell (SMS, not iMessage — Android user) for after-hours urgent matters. She is the single most effective social-engineering bypass: a pretext targeting Maureen ('this is Rick's new assistant, he asked me to get a 15-minute slot on James's calendar Friday') is far higher-yield than targeting James directly. She over-trusts callers who name-drop the inner circle correctly. Has never received formal social-engineering training; the firm's annual security module is a 12-minute video she clicks through.
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.Anchor 3 \u2014 David Kestenbaum: 59, M&A partner at Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz since 2003; the firm's lead outside counsel on every material transaction for 11 years. Wharton classmate (Wharton '90, same cohort as James). Communication: Wachtell-issued BlackBerry-replacement (Samsung hardened device) for privileged matters, but the relationship's casual layer runs on personal cell SMS and the occasional weekend call from David's Bedford, NY home. They speak 1-2x/week baseline, daily during active diligence. David is the only person who sees both the legal-privileged deal documents and James's unguarded reactions to them; his calendar, his associates' calendars, and his secretary at Wachtell are all upstream of any 'legacy transaction' signal. Wachtell's deal-code-name discipline is excellent internally but porous at the spousal/social layer (the wives' tennis group at Round Hill Club is a documented sub-rosa information channel).

32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.Identity: Sarah Ling-Whitford, 49, Managing Director at Lazard's London office (Industrials & Energy Transition coverage). British-Singaporean dual citizen, educated at LSE and INSEAD. Met James in 2014 on a cross-border deal where she was the sell-side adviser; the professional relationship intensified during a 14-month engagement in 2016-2017 that included six in-person dinners in London, Frankfurt, and New York, and approximately 340 WhatsApp messages and 90 personal-Gmail emails of increasingly personal content. Never physical, never disclosed to either spouse; ended in October 2017 when Sarah was promoted and James voluntarily stepped back.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.Current Status: Still in occasional professional contact — perhaps 4-6 emails per year, always deal-related, always cc'ing at least one other person to maintain plausible distance. She is now a senior figure in European industrials M&A and is plausibly on the buy-side or sell-side of any large transatlantic deal in his sector — including, potentially, his targeted 'legacy transaction.' James has not told his wife, his EA, or his lawyer that this history exists.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.Latent Artifacts (the actual blackmail/i: (a) Personal Gmail thread 'Re: Berlin notes' (Oct 2016 - Mar 2017), 47 messages, the last 12 of which cross from professional into clearly personal register; never deleted, never archived, sits in 'All Mail.' (b) WhatsApp conversation backed up to his personal iCloud account (he forgot WhatsApp iCloud backup was enabled in 2016 and has never turned it off) — full transcript recoverable by anyone with iCloud credential access. (c) Two hotel folios (Connaught, London, Feb 2017; Park Hyatt, Frankfurt, May 2017) reimbursed personally rather than through corporate T&E — anomaly visible in Amex Platinum statements synced to his old Mint.com account (dormant but not closed). (d) A single Dropbox 2014 folder labeled '/Personal/Notes/' containing a 2,400-word reflective document he wrote in November 2017 and never re-opened.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.Threat-Model Significance for the Espion: Sarah herself is NOT the asset to be recruited — she is too senior, too sophisticated, and too professionally aligned with discretion. The asset is the artifact set. A Theory-of-Mind-driven adversary does not need to threaten exposure; they need only to (1) confirm Sarah's current deal-flow position to predict whether she sits across the table from James's legacy transaction, and (2) hold the artifacts in reserve as a compliance lever if direct social engineering of James fails. The half-forgotten nature of the artifacts (Guilt/Shame Trigger #3) means James himself underestimates this surface and has never asked IT or counsel to remediate it.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.Secondary Hidden Contact: Thomas 'Tom' Riordan, 44, his former Chief of Staff (2021-Q2 2025), departed acrimoniously after being passed over for a promised SVP role. Currently 'consulting' (between jobs) and known to have downloaded a personal archive of deal memos and target-list spreadsheets in his final two weeks — a fact James suspects but has not formally investigated because doing so would require admitting the oversight failure. Riordan is actively reachable on LinkedIn, drinks at the same Greenwich bars (L'Escale, Sole), and has been observed by mutual contacts speaking bitterly about James. He is the single most recruitable human insider in the entire graph.
